title: Codegen: implementation-independent generated command packages
larger_intended_outcome: Generated command implementations should be disposable projections from implementation-independent artifacts that define behavior, data, interfaces, and conformance expectations once. Tests should treat every generated implementation as a black-box target reached through a thin handwritten adapter.
non_goals: Do not make generated code the behavioral source of truth.
Do not mirror package behavior inside target adapters.
Do not combine unrelated implementation lanes into one execplan.
Do not publish or recommend immature generated targets without explicit maturity signaling.

candidate_lanes.title: Canonical artifact model
candidate_lanes.outcome: Define the implementation-independent operation, interface, data, and fixture surfaces that generated command targets consume.
candidate_lanes.proof: Default generated-package static proof validates that each generated command declares conformance refs and projection-boundary ownership across universal, target-specific, and runtime-owned surfaces.

candidate_lanes.title: Black-box conformance harness
candidate_lanes.outcome: Run shared behavior and output tests through target adapters from checked-in conformance contract artifacts instead of generated implementation internals or handwritten proof-case mirrors.
candidate_lanes.proof: The generated TypeScript conformance proof derives command argv, fixture files, and expected output fields from conformance/*.json for root, Planning, and Memory targets.

candidate_lanes.title: Thin target adapters
candidate_lanes.outcome: Specify and implement narrow language- or transport-specific adapters that translate shared test input into CLI, shell, MCP, or package API invocation without duplicating package behavior.
candidate_lanes.proof: Adapter review and tests show adapters contain invocation translation only, with no copied command behavior or generated package logic.

candidate_lanes.title: Generated target maturity
candidate_lanes.outcome: Harden Python and TypeScript generated command package targets enough for evaluation and weak-agent routing while keeping maturity status explicit.
candidate_lanes.proof: Targeted generated-package tests, source-checkout invocation proof, and maturity-report checks pass for promoted targets.

candidate_lanes.title: Additional transports
candidate_lanes.outcome: Evaluate shell, PowerShell, MCP, or package API projections only after the canonical model and harness are stable.
candidate_lanes.proof: New target candidates pass the same black-box conformance expectations through thin adapters before being promoted.

candidate_lanes.title: Drift and release gates
candidate_lanes.outcome: Make stale generated output, duplicated behavior, adapter bloat, and target maturity regressions visible in ordinary proof.
candidate_lanes.proof: Default generated-package static proof fails when runnable TypeScript targets lose contract-derived conformance coverage or reintroduce unsafe runtime handoff shelling.

dependency_assumptions: #880 is closed; root, Planning, and Memory TypeScript generated CLI targets are weak-agent-safe for read-only routing.
Black-box conformance can consume the existing conformance contract artifacts for the current generated TypeScript targets before broader canonical artifact redesign.
Additional transports should wait until adapters and shared conformance tests prove the target contract.
parallelization_assumptions: Adapter contract shaping can overlap with harness design after the canonical artifact model is clear.

Target maturity hardening can proceed for existing generated Python and TypeScript targets while larger canonical model work is being shaped, as long as it does not introduce another behavioral source of truth.
proof_expectations: Each promoted lane records narrow validation in its execplan.
Behavior proof should prefer shared black-box conformance fixtures over generated-code internals.
Generated code should be treated as disposable output and regenerated from canonical artifacts during proof where practical.
Adapters should be reviewed for thinness and absence of mirrored package behavior.
promotion_rule: Promote only lanes with clear outcome, owner surface, proof expectation, bounded write scope, and a stated relationship to GitHub epic #892 and milestone 1.
notes: This decomposition is the epic-level shape for the codegen project. It is not an execution journal and should not collect historical lane records after slices close.
